title: Measuring Droplet Volume in Home-Made Microfluidic Devices
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"90c45","text":"Drop-seq is a technology we developed to enable biologists to analyze RNA expression genome-wide in thousands of individual cells at once.  We first described this in a 2015 paper in Cell.  Though commercial implementations of droplet-based single-cell RNA-seq also now exist, we have made Drop-seq open-source and want to make sure that any lab can build their own system.  The materials for constructing a Drop-seq setup in one’s own lab can be obtained for about $6,000. The reagents for performing Drop-seq cost about 6 cents per cell.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":183,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":167,"length":20}],"data":[]},{"key":"4rtr7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1hlil","text":"This is a supplemental protocol of our Drop-Seq Protocol for measuring droplet volume in home-made microfluidic devices. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":39,"length":17}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7A44B7E86A404BA5AF814B972F0B24C7","url":"http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0092867415005498"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"808760606D6343278EA03B6ED59D075D","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/drop-seq-laboratory-protocol-mkbc4sn"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"csq1e","text":" Videos and FAQs ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fi7qc","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66jol","text":"These tutorials, images, and diagrams may be helpful in building your own Drop-seq setup and doing Drop-seq experiments in your lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":6,"length":31}],"data":[]},{"key":"8bmns","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"beuro","text":"We have also created a YouTube channel with a variety of videos to help scientists through the steps that most benefit from watching.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":57,"length":6}],"data":[]},{"key":"2hkf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ehar","text":"This FAQ provides also provides supplementary information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":5,"length":3}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"1FEE3C24ADA34B9B890234EA1C617251","url":"http://mccarrolllab.com/drop-seq-troubleshootingtutorial-with-pictures-videos/"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"DED36275790E4EB3BC5B7F1427DAEF33","url":"https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCRptwggZzFyM51R5iAI-07Q"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C4400F01EE2A49379957E6FC77795E08","url":"http://mccarrolllab.com/drop-seq-faq/"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"82lsi","text":"To measure droplet volume, purchase some durable, monodisperse polystyrene beads with a hydrophilic coating (e.x. 10­‐micron carboxylated polystyrene beads from Bangs Labs, product #PC06N-11355. It can be helpful to use fluorescent beads to be sure you can identify them in droplets. Bangs cells these under product # FC06F‐10163).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89m9a","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
